GLOBAL DEFENCE SPENDING HITS 16-YEAR HIGH

A new report by Stockholm International Peace Research Institute shows global military spending surged to $2.9 trillion in 2025, the highest share of global GDP since 2009.

Highlights:
- Europe (+14%) and Asia (+8.1%) drove the spike
- Top spenders: U.S., China, Russia, Germany, India (58% of total)
- India spent ~$92.1B, up 8.9%

Rising tensions and ongoing conflicts are pushing nations toward long-term military expansion, with spending expected to keep climbing into 2026 and beyond.

tweets.title:
GLOBAL DEFENCE SPENDING HITS 16-YEAR HIGH

A new report by Stockholm International Peace Research Institute shows global military spending surged to $2.9 trillion in 2025, the highest share of global GDP since 2009.

Highlights:
- Europe (+14%) and Asia (+8.1%) drove the spike
- Top spenders: U.S., China, Russia, Germany, India (58% of total)
- India spent ~$92.1B, up 8.9%

Rising tensions and ongoing conflicts are pushing nations toward long-term military expansion, with spending expected to keep climbing into 2026 and beyond.

Iran Sends New Peace Proposal to U.S. via Pakistan

Iran has reportedly submitted a “new proposal” to the United States through Pakistani intermediaries, aiming to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end the war, according to reports.

 Key points of the proposal:
- Two-stage plan; first restore maritime flow & ease blockade
- Option of extended ceasefire or permanent end to conflict
- Nuclear talks to be deferred until after Hormuz is reopened

Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi reportedly told mediators there is no internal consensus yet on U.S. demands like halting uranium enrichment.
